revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Amatsukami]][[fr:Amatsukami]][[it:Amatsukami]][[mni:ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ]][[zh:天神 (日本神話)]]
{{Short description|Shinto sky and heaven kami|qq=}}
{{italic title|qq=}}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-en.svg|frame]]
{{Nihongo|'''''Amatsukami'''''|天津神, 天つ神|extra='[[Kami]] of heaven'|lead=yes|qq=}} is a Category of [[kami]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. Generally speaking, it refers to kami born in, or residing in, [[Takamagahara]].
''Amatsukami'' is one of the three categories of kami, along with their earthly counterpart {{Nihongo|''[[Kunitsukami]]''|国津神, 国つ神|qq=}}, and {{Nihongo|''yaoyorozu-no-kami''|八百万の神|qq=}}.<ref name="Yamakage"/>{{rp|56|qq=}}
In the time of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] the ownership of land was moved from [[Kunitsukami]] to Amatsukami.
==Mythology==
{{Off topic|date=October 2021|qq=}}
'''Amatsukami'' refers to kami residing in Takamagahara, along with kami who were born in Takamagahara but later descended to {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref name="EOS"/> In the mythological event of [[kuni-yuzuri]], the descendants of ''amatsukami'' descended to pacify the world, which was occupied by the ''kunitsukami''.<ref name="Bocking2005"/><ref name="EOS2"/> 
[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], who was cast out of [[Takamagahara]], and his descendants, such as [[Ōkuninushi]], are considered to be Kunitsugami.
==List of ''amatsukami''==
{{Unreferenced section|date=September 2021|qq=}}
*[[Kotoamatsukami]]
** [[Ame-no-Minakanushi|Amenominakanushi]]
** [[Takamimusubi]]
** [[Kamimusubi]]
** [[Umashi-Ashikabi-Hikoji|Umashiashikabihikoji]]
** [[Ame-no-Tokotachi]]
*[[Kamiyonanayo]]
**[[Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi]]
**{{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumonu|ja|トヨクモノ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu|qq=}}
**{{ill|Uhijini and Suhijini|lt=Suhijini and Uhijini|ja|ウヒヂニ・スヒヂニ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uhijini and Suhijini|qq=}}
**{{ill|Tsunugui and Ikugui|lt=Tsunuguhi and Ikuguhi|ja|オオトノヂ・オオトノベ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunugui and Ikugui|qq=}}
**{{ill|Otonoji and Otonobe|ja|オオトノヂ・オオトノベ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otonoji and Otonobe|qq=}}
**{{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|ja|オモダル・アヤカシコネ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omodaru and Ayakashikone|qq=}}
**[[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}
*Shusaishin
**[[Amaterasu]]
*Others
**[[Ame no Hohi]]
**[[Ame-no-Koyane]]
**{{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|qid=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
**{{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|qid=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}
**[[Ame-no-Uzume]]
**[[Ame no Wakahiko]]
**[[Futodama]]
**[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]
**[[Omoikane|Omoikane]]
**[[Takemikazuchi]]
**{{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|en|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|qid=Q11572729|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}}
== See also ==
* [[:en:Aesir|Aesir]] and {{ill|Vanir|en|Vanir|qid=Q184826|lt=Vanir|1=Vanir}}
* [[Tsumi|Heavenly and Earthly crimes]]
==References==
{{reflist|
refs=
<ref name="Bocking2005">{{cite book|author=Brian Bocking|title=A Popular Dictionary of Shinto|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6d-RAgAAQBAJ|date=30 September 2005|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-135-79739-3|page=7|qq=}}</ref>
<ref name="EOS">{{cite web |url=http://k-amc.kokugakuin.ac.jp/EOS/22214 |title=Amatsukami, Kunitsukami |last=Endō |first=Jun |work=國學院デジタルミュージアム |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]]|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210116222631/http://k-amc.kokugakuin.ac.jp/DM/detail.do?class_name=col_eos&data_id=22214 |archive-date=2021-01-16 |accessdate=2021-01-16 |qq=}}</ref>
<ref name="EOS2">{{cite web|last1=Kobori|first1=Keiko|title=Kuniyuzuri|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1210|website=Encyclopedia of Shinto|publisher=Kokugakuin University|accessdate=2021-01-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210116233825/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1210 |archive-date=2021-01-16 |qq=}}</ref>
<ref name="Yamakage">{{cite book|last1=Yamakage|first1=Motohisa|last2=Gillespie|first2=Mineko S.|last3=Gillespie|first3=Gerald L.|last4=Komuro|first4=Yoshitsugu|last5=Leeuw|first5=Paul de|last6=Rankin|first6=Aidan|title=The Essence of Shinto: Japan's Spiritual Heart|date=2007|publisher=Kodansha International|location=Tokyo|isbn=978-4770030443|edition=1st|url-access=registration|url=https://archive.org/details/essenceofshintoj0000yama|qq=}}</ref>
}}{{Jmyth navbox long|qq=}}
